response.justification: The candidate is a capable, versatile full-stack engineer with strong distributed-systems exposure (low-latency microservices at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/sec) and founder-level ownership of an open-source geo-data platform. However, the role's core minimum bar is 8 years of C++ and large-scale cloud compute platform work, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint (~6 months at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis). The bulk of the experience is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with no internal cloud compute platform or formal cloud/DevOps technical-leadership experience. The gap against the hard requirements is too large for this specific role.
strengths: Distributed-systems experience with high-throughput, low-latency microservices
Founder-level ownership and architecture of an open-source platform deployable on Kubernetes
Strong engineering-practice mindset (TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, talks)
concerns: Far short of the 8-year C++ requirement; only minimal professional C/C++ experience
No internal cloud compute platform or production cloud-architecture leadership
No formal multi-year technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers
